reasoning: The crowd forecast reflects an overwhelming consensus (1%) that the US Congress will not pass substantive, binding AI regulation before January 2026. Substantive AI regulation is highly complex, politically contentious, and lacks bipartisan consensus for strict, EU-style binding requirements on AI development or capabilities. Lawmakers have primarily focused on narrow issues (like deepfakes) or non-binding research initiatives (like the CREATE AI Act), which the resolution criteria explicitly rule out. Given the heavily divided Congress, fears of stifling technological innovation and losing pace with China, and the extremely short amount of time left to negotiate, draft, pass, and enact a comprehensive AI regulatory framework, the probability remains exceptionally low. I am matching the lowest allowed probability within the system bounds (0.02) to reflect the near-zero chance of such legislation being enacted before the deadline.
